question: Find the city the Southwest district of the Missouri Department of Transportation is based in and a highway that was born in the city. Between that highway's two terminus cities, which one had higher binge drinking rates in 2016 and 2018? For that city, identify the department with the second-highest overtime costs from 2014 to 2017 and state its founding year. Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Missouri Department of Transportation, 'MoDOT operates seven districts throughout the state: Northwest, based in St. Joseph; Northeast, based in Hannibal; Kansas City, based in Lee's Summit; Central, based in Jefferson City; St. Louis, based in Chesterfield; Southwest, based in Springfield; and Southeast, based in Sikeston.'
1.subquestion: In which city is the Southwest district of the Missouri Department of Transportation based?

2.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Springfield, Missouri, 'Springfield's nicknames include "Queen City of the Ozarks" and "The Birthplace of Route 66"...Officially recognized as the birthplace of Route 66, it was in Springfield on April 30, 1926, that officials first proposed the name of the new Chicago-to-Los Angeles highway.'
2.subquestion: What are the terminus cities of the highway that was born in <node_1 answer>?

3.fact: In the 500-Cities 2016 health data release, the binge drinking prevalence (city-level, crude) was: Chicago 20.1% and Los Angeles 15.9%. Chicago had higher binge drinking prevalence.
3.subquestion: Which terminus city (from <node_2 answer>) had higher average binge drinking prevalence across the 2016 and 2018 500-Cities health releases? (Filter for this node: Short_Question_Text == 'Binge Drinking'; GeographicLevel == 'City'; Data_Value_Type == 'Crude prevalence'; CityName in {Chicago, Los Angeles}; use Data_Value.)

4.fact: In the 500-Cities 2018 health data release, the binge drinking prevalence (city-level, crude) was: Chicago 21.5% and Los Angeles 16.7%. Chicago had higher binge drinking prevalence.
4.subquestion: Which terminus city (from <node_2 answer>) had higher average binge drinking prevalence across the 2016 and 2018 500-Cities health releases? (Filter for this node: Short_Question_Text == 'Binge Drinking'; GeographicLevel == 'City'; Data_Value_Type == 'Crude prevalence'; CityName in {Chicago, Los Angeles}; use Data_Value.)

5.fact: In the 2014 Chicago Employee Overtime data, the top departments by total overtime were: Police ($101,460,132.24), Fire ($63,941,565.36), Water Management ($23,507,061.70), Streets and Sanitation ($15,393,693.24), and Aviation ($13,147,383.40). Fire had the 2nd highest.
5.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from 2014-2017 in the city identified from the binge drinking comparison? (Filter for this node: group by DEPARTMENT NAME; sum TOTAL; rank by total; keep top departments.)

6.fact: In the 2015 Chicago Employee Overtime data, the top departments by total overtime were: Police ($116,144,553.32), Fire ($43,847,384.30), Water Management ($19,954,056.68), Streets and Sanitation ($16,582,331.52). Fire had the 2nd highest.
6.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from 2014-2017 in the city identified from the binge drinking comparison? (Filter for this node: group by DEPARTMENT NAME; sum TOTAL; rank by total; keep top departments.)

7.fact: In the 2016 Chicago Employee Overtime data, the top departments by total overtime were: Police ($143,032,724.45), Fire ($50,604,010.67), Water Management ($21,335,912.52), Streets and Sanitation ($13,836,742.94). Fire had the 2nd highest.
7.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from 2014-2017 in the city identified from the binge drinking comparison? (Filter for this node: group by DEPARTMENT NAME; sum TOTAL; rank by total; keep top departments.)

8.fact: In the 2017 Chicago Employee Overtime data, the top departments by total overtime were: Police ($156,732,988), Fire ($49,571,354), Water Management ($20,049,910), Aviation ($14,884,756). Fire had the 2nd highest. Fire was consistently 2nd across all 4 years (2014-2017).
8.subquestion: Which city department had the 2nd highest total overtime from 2014-2017 in the city identified from the binge drinking comparison? (Filter for this node: group by DEPARTMENT NAME; sum TOTAL; rank by total; keep top departments.)

9.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Chicago Fire Department, 'The volunteer fire department was disestablished on August 2, 1858, when the city council passed the ordinance organizing the paid City of Chicago Fire Department.' The department was founded in 1858.
9.subquestion: In what year was the department identified in the overtime comparison founded?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: MoDOT Southwest district is based in Springfield
Node 2: Route 66 was the Chicago-to-Los Angeles highway
Node 3: 2016 binge drinking - Chicago 20.1%, LA 15.9% -> Chicago higher
Node 4: 2018 binge drinking - Chicago 21.5%, LA 16.7% -> Chicago higher
Node 9: Chicago Fire Department founded in 1858

reasoning_hops.reasoning: Node 1: MoDOT Southwest district is based in Springfield
Node 2: Route 66 was the Chicago-to-Los Angeles highway
Node 3: 2016 binge drinking - Chicago 20.1%, LA 15.9% -> Chicago higher Node 4: 2018 binge drinking - Chicago 21.5%, LA 16.7% -> Chicago higher Result: Chicago
Node 5: 2014 overtime -> Police #1, Fire #2 Node 6: 2015 overtime -> Police #1, Fire #2 Node 7: 2016 overtime -> Police #1, Fire #2 Node 8: 2017 overtime -> Police #1, Fire #2 Result: Fire
Node 9: Chicago Fire Department founded in 1858
